dosing: Typical prescription doses range from 600 mg to 1500 mg per day of lithium carbonate.
The dose is titrated upward until therapeutic blood levels are reached, with regular blood level monitoring required.
Some users report trying low-dose lithium orotate as an over-the-counter supplement alternative.
preparations: Prescription lithium carbonate in oral tablet form is the standard preparation.
Lithium orotate is available as an OTC supplement but has limited evidence for cluster headache.
schedule: Taken daily as an ongoing preventive during active cycles or chronic periods.
Often combined with verapamil as a dual preventive regimen.
Must be discontinued before psilocybin or LSD busting due to dangerous seizure risk from the interaction.
